about.title: About RepoPulse
about.description: RepoPulse is an industry-grade, high-performance dashboard designed for developers and DevOps teams to monitor software releases across multiple platforms in real-time. It provides a centralized hub for tracking GitHub, GitLab, and other source providers with advanced filtering, categorization, and automation features.
about.feature1: Universal Source Tracking (GitHub, GitLab, Gitea, Forgejo)
about.creditsDesc: RepoPulse was engineered by nexgen with the power of advanced AI agents. This collaboration represents the next generation of software development, blending human high-level design with AI-driven execution speed.
about.step1: Extract Payload: In AI Studio, go to Settings > Export to ZIP to get the full source.
about.step2: Environment Setup: Ensure Node.js (v18+) is installed on your server or local machine.
about.step3: Dependency Resolution: Navigate to the project folder and run 'npm install'.
about.step4: Production Build: Compile the optimized assets using 'npm run build'.
about.step5: Launch: Serve the 'dist' folder using an engine like Nginx, Apache, or 'npx serve dist'.
about.note: Security & Connectivity: RepoPulse operates entirely on your infrastructure. Ensure your network has access to GitHub/GitLab APIs. Personal Access Tokens are stored securely in your browser's persistent storage.
about.version: RepoPulse Dashboard v1.7.0 • Refined by nexgen & AI

changelog.title: Changelog & Evolution
changelog.subtitle: The journey of RepoPulse from prototype to production.
changelog.v17: Enterprise Refinement: Implemented robust 'Uncategorized' default logic for all import streams (User, Stars, Bulk, Manual). Overhauled Wiki and Documentation with detailed guides. Major translation upgrade across 15+ languages. Optimized Explorer icon stability and removed obsolete protocol handlers. Refactored Changelog and About interfaces for better readability.
changelog.v16: UX & Modernization: Added right-click context menu for repositories. Enhanced Wiki with full documentation. Upgraded translations and updated versioning. Refined backup logic and UI consistency. Added File Explorer mode for supported sources.
changelog.v15: Feature Expansion: Added quick search, drag-and-drop for repos and categories, favorites system, and improved category editing. Optimized backup structure and icon storage.
changelog.v14: UX & Layout Optimization: Implemented resizable sidebar. Major overhaul of Repository Manager with full-height categories. Added custom scrollbars and improved visual hierarchy.
changelog.v13: Full-Stack Persistence: Migrated to Express backend. Implemented inline repository editing. Updated default category structure for gaming and emulation. Fixed sub-category navigation bugs.
changelog.v12: Customization Overhaul: Fully customizable theme with color picker. Font selection moved to dropdown. Dashboard view switcher added to top bar. Performance optimizations.
changelog.v11: Advanced Visualization: Added 'Stats' view with Recharts. Implemented 'Bento Grid' and 'Columns' layouts. Added new professional themes: Midnight, Forest, Sunset, Ocean.
changelog.v10: Internationalization: Full multi-language support (15+ languages). Advanced backup system and custom icon upload support.
changelog.v05: OS Filtering: Added compatibility tags (Windows, Linux, macOS) and advanced filtering logic.
changelog.v04: Fluent Design: Major UI overhaul with Microsoft's Fluent UI principles. Performance optimizations for large repo lists.
changelog.v03: Nested Categories: Implemented up to 5 levels of category depth. Improved UI responsiveness.
changelog.v02: GitLab Integration: Support for GitLab.com and self-hosted instances. Basic category system.
changelog.v01: Initial build: Core architecture, basic GitHub release tracking, and simple repository management.

wiki.title: Wiki & Documentation
wiki.subtitle: A comprehensive guide to mastering RepoPulse.
wiki.dashboard: Dashboard: Your mission control for software updates. Filter by categories, OS tags, or use the instant search.
wiki.repos: Repositories: Connect GitHub/GitLab sources. Use tokens for private access and higher rate limits.
wiki.categories: Categories: Architect your workspace with infinite flexibility (up to 5 nesting levels).
wiki.backups: Data & Backups: Manage your persistent storage, exports, and automated synchronization.
wiki.settings: Configuration: Tune the engine—themes, languages, refresh intervals, and API credentials.
wiki.dashboardDetail1: Smart Filtering: Multi-select categories via the sidebar to find exactly what you need.
wiki.dashboardDetail2: Release Intelligence: View version diffs and full markdown notes without leaving the app.
wiki.dashboardDetail3: Layout Engine: Switch between List, Grid, Bento, and Columns to suit your screen real estate.
wiki.dashboardDetail4: Contextual Power: Right-click any repository for rapid actions (Favorite, Refresh, Backup).
wiki.reposDetail1: GitHub/GitLab: Integrated support for major git providers and self-hosted instances.
wiki.reposDetail2: Source Management: Bulk import whole user profiles or starred projects in seconds.
wiki.reposDetail3: Auto-Backup Sync: Enable on a per-repo basis to keep a local archive of every release.
wiki.reposDetail4: Metadata Tagging: Assign OS tags to repos for precise cross-platform filtering.
wiki.categoriesDetail1: Logical Nesting: Create deep hierarchies (e.g., Gaming > Emulators > Nintendo > Switch).
wiki.categoriesDetail2: Custom Visuals: Upload PNG/ICO icons to make categories instantly recognizable.
wiki.categoriesDetail3: Color Identity: Assign specific color accents to different category branches.
wiki.categoriesDetail4: Fluid Reordering: Drag and drop categories in the manager to rethink your structure.
wiki.backupsDetail1: Export Logic: Full data export in JSON format for backups or migration.
wiki.backupsDetail2: File Organization: Downloads are nested by Category > Repo > Version for total order.
wiki.backupsDetail3: State Persistence: All changes are saved in real-time to the 'user/' backend directory.
wiki.backupsDetail4: Browser Integrity: Native download handling respects your browser's security model.
wiki.settingsDetail1: Global Reach: Optimized translations for 15+ regions and languages.
wiki.settingsDetail2: Theme Designer: Go beyond Light/Dark with custom accent colors and blur effects.
wiki.settingsDetail3: API Security: Your tokens are stored locally and never transmitted to our servers.
wiki.settingsDetail4: Refresh Intervals: Control how often RepoPulse checks for updates, from 15m to 24h.
wiki.webhooks: Integrations: (Upcoming) Support for Discord and Slack update notifications.
wiki.docIntro: Welcome to the official RepoPulse documentation. This guide provides deep-dive instructions for each module.
wiki.docDashboard: Dashboard: The primary viewport. Use the resizable sidebar to navigate categories. The top bar contains global search and layout switches. Each repository card shows the latest release version, date, and description. Click a card to expand its full history. Pro Tip: Use the Context Menu (Right-Click) for rapid management of favorites and backups.
wiki.docExplorer: File Explorer: A powerful dual-pane storage manager. (1) Support for all local drives (C:, D:, etc.) across platforms. (2) Full address bar with navigation capabilities. (3) Context Menu (Right-Click) containing Copy Path, Refresh, Size/Date Sorting, and multiple View Modes (Icons, List, Details). (4) Robust file operations including transferring between panes, renaming, and multi-file deletion.
wiki.docFavorites: Favorites: Click the star on any repository to pin it here. This view allows you to stay focused on your most critical projects without category noise.
wiki.docRepoManager: Repo Manager: The backend control. Add repositories via URL. Pro Tip: Use the 'GitHub User Import' in Settings > Data to fetch your entire profile at once. These will default to 'Uncategorized' for your sorting convenience.
wiki.docCategories: Categories: Located in Settings > Structure. This view allows you to manage the backbone of your dashboard. You can add, edit, or delete categories. Dragging a category into another makes it a sub-category.
wiki.docBackups: Data Management: RepoPulse uses a full-stack local persistence layer. Your data is stored in the 'user/' folder of the server. Use the Import/Export tools to move your configuration between devices.
wiki.docSettings: Settings: Configure your Personal Access Token here. Without a token, GitHub limits requests severely. A token allows for 5,000 requests per hour and private repo access.
